question: For the `Book` class (from a previous problem), overload the output stream operator `<<` to allow printing `Book` objects directly to `std::cout` in a formatted way (e.g., "Title: [title], Author: [author], ISBN: [isbn]").

test_cases.expected:
Printing book1 (non-const Book&, stream returned by value):
Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy, Author: Douglas Adams, ISBN: 978-0345391803

Printing book2 with chaining attempt:
Title: 1984, Author: George Orwell, ISBN: 978-0451524935 - A classic dystopian novel.

errors: The overloaded output stream operator takes the 'Book' object by non-const reference (`Book& book`). This violates const-correctness, as an output operation should not modify the object being printed, making the design less robust against unintended side effects.

The overloaded output stream operator returns `std::ostream` by value instead of `std::ostream&`. This breaks the standard mechanism for stream chaining, as subsequent `<<` operations would apply to a temporary copy of the stream, potentially leading to unexpected behavior or loss of stream state modifications in more complex scenarios.
